Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Hardware hotplug events on Linux, the gory details 37 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Optimizing Recommendation Systems with JDK's Vector API 39 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Show HN: Jido 2.0, Elixir Agent Framework 180 upvotes, 39 comments
  4. Let's Get Physical 33 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. A GitHub Issue Title Compromised 4k Developer Machines 178 upvotes, 43 comments
  6. Wikipedia in read-only mode following mass admin account compromise 651 upvotes, 196 comments
  7. Google Workspace CLI 863 upvotes, 272 comments
  8. Building a new Flash 695 upvotes, 226 comments
  9. Google Safe Browsing missed 84% of confirmed phishing sites 233 upvotes, 69 comments
  10. Poor Man's Polaroid 168 upvotes, 48 comments
  11. Datasets for Reconstructing Visual Perception from Brain Data 34 upvotes, 6 comments
  12. Nvidia PersonaPlex 7B on Apple Silicon: Full-Duplex Speech-to-Speech in Swift 332 upvotes, 110 comments

  14. OpenTitan Shipping in Production 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Fast-Servers 81 upvotes, 25 comments
  16. The Government Uses Targeted Advertising to Track Your Location 177 upvotes, 66 comments
  17. Remotely unlocking an encrypted hard disk 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  18. World-first gigabit laser link between aircraft and geostationary satellite 142 upvotes, 53 comments
  19. AI and the Ship of Theseus 24 upvotes, 6 comments
  20. Smalltalk's Browser: Unbeatable, yet Not Enough 123 upvotes, 61 comments
  21. Good software knows when to stop 245 upvotes, 141 comments
  22. Greg Kroah-Hartman Stretches Support Periods for Key Linux LTS Kernels 41 upvotes, 17 comments
  23. A man who broke into jail 73 upvotes, 36 comments
  24. Show HN: PageAgent, A GUI agent that lives inside your web app 46 upvotes, 26 comments
  25. The Brand Age 85 upvotes, 64 comments
  26. GPT-5.4 282 upvotes, 264 comments
  27. AMD will bring its “Ryzen AI” processors to standard desktop PCs for first time 204 upvotes, 191 comments
  28. Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite 341 upvotes, 337 comments
